title: notifications core — Eloquent observers
description: Model observers that live in this module. They translate lifecycle hooks into events + cache invalidations + audit rows. Each observer is registered in NotificationsServiceProvider via `Model::observe(ObserverClass::class)`.

handles: created → fire NotificationDispatched (only if state=queued at persist time)
updated (state=sent) → fire NotificationSent
updated (state=delivered) → fire NotificationDelivered
updated (state=failed) → fire NotificationFailed
updated (seen_at set) → fire NotificationSeen + invalidate user's unseen-count cache
updated (archived_at set) → fire NotificationArchived
deleting → refuse when is_system=true unless actor is super_admin
created → fire NotificationQueued for observability
updated (state transitions) → update parent Notification.state via a small state-projection routine (Delivery state is per-channel; Notification.state is the aggregate)
updated (opened_at set) → fire NotificationOpened
updated (last_click_at set) → fire NotificationClicked
created → write audit row with template_key + version + channel + locale (SOC 2 CC8.1 change management evidence)
updated (state → published) → fire TemplatePublished
updated (state → archived) → invalidate template registry cache
deleting → refuse when is_system=true unless actor is super_admin
deleting → refuse when Notification.template_id references this row + has state≠[archived,failed] (would orphan live sends)
created → fire PreferenceUpdated with source=user_settings (or from listener source)
updated → fire PreferenceUpdated + invalidate user's preference cache
deleted → fire PreferenceUpdated with enabled=false (audit trail of the removal)
created → fire CategoryRegistered
updated → invalidate category registry cache
deleting → refuse when NotificationTemplate rows reference this slug OR when Notification rows referenced this slug in last 90d
